metrics.name: Market solvency
metrics.desc:
Total supplied,~$372.03M USDC
Total borrowed,~$259.83M USDC
Available cash / market liquidity,~$126.16M USDC
Utilization rate,69.85%
Total reserves,Reserve buffer exists, precise current value not stated in past evidence
Reserve factor,Not stated in past evidence
Collateral composition backing USDC borrows,WBTC, ETH, wstETH, weETH, cbBTC, tBTC, LINK, COMP, UNI, rsETH, USDe
Share of debt by collateral,WBTC 55.4%, ETH 22.3%, wstETH 10.9%, weETH 5.3%, cbBTC 3.8%, tBTC 1.1%, others ~1.2%
Concentration in top collateral assets,Top 4 collateral assets ~93.84% of posted collateral
Exposure to correlated collateral,BTC-linked collateral ~60.30%; ETH/LST/LRT-linked collateral ~38.49%
Risk classification per collateral,Mostly blue-chip BTC/ETH assets and large liquid majors
Liquidity profile of each collateral,Strong for the dominant BTC/ETH collateral set
Dependence on illiquid or long-tail collateral,Low
Distribution of borrower sizes,Not publicly disclosed, although historical Gauntlet analysis identified whale-sized positions
Liquidation thresholds / collateral factors by collateral,WBTC 85%, ETH 88%, wstETH 86%, weETH 80%, cbBTC 85%, tBTC 85%, LINK 79%, COMP 70%, UNI 74%, rsETH 90%, USDe 94%
Maximum LTV per collateral,WBTC 80%, ETH 83%, wstETH 82%, weETH 75%, cbBTC 80%, tBTC 80%, LINK 73%, COMP 50%, UNI 68%, rsETH 85%, USDe 89%
Liquidation incentives,Asset-specific liquidation penalties range from 4% to 25%
Close factor,Not stated in past evidence
Existence of caps, isolation, or equivalent constraints,Yes, isolated single-base-asset market and per-asset collateral supply caps
Historical bad debt in the market,No material bad debt specifically reported for the Ethereum USDC market
Evidence of solvency during prior stress events,Yes, Aug. 2024 and Oct. 2025 downturn analyses showed absorption activity without reported reserve impairment
Collateral stress test evidence,Yes, Gauntlet 2024 liquidation review modeled delayed collateral sales and potential reserve stress under additional 10–30% price declines

metrics.name: Market solvency
metrics.desc:
The market is large, overcollateralized, and supported by meaningful available USDC liquidity.
Collateral concentration is high, but the dominant collateral is mostly BTC/ETH-linked rather than illiquid long-tail assets.
Stress-event evidence is favorable, although liquidation execution and correlated collateral moves remain the main solvency risks.

metrics.name: Withdrawal liquidity
metrics.desc:
Current utilization,69.85%
Historical average utilization,Recent 2025 data ranged roughly from the low-70s to around 90%
Historical peak utilization,At least 97.71% observed in the 2024-01-04 to 2024-01-10 weekly update, ending that week at 95.43%
Available cash ratio,~33.91% of current earning base balances
Immediate withdrawal capacity,~$126.16M USDC
Borrow concentration,Not publicly disclosed in the retrieved official sources
Dependence on repayments for withdrawals,Yes
Dependence on liquidations for withdrawals,Yes in stress
Evidence of withdrawal friction during stress,Yes, Nov. 2025 governance / guardian action paused withdrawals of existing liquidity and collateral supply during deUSD / sdeUSD-related stress
Liquidity management mechanisms,Utilization-based interest-rate curve, base-asset reserves, and discounted collateral sales via `buyCollateral`
Evidence of persistent liquidity shortages,Not found, although utilization has periodically been very high

metrics.name: Withdrawal liquidity
metrics.desc:
Normal-state liquidity is meaningful, with more than $126M immediately available.
Historical utilization has sometimes been extremely tight, showing that withdrawal liquidity can deteriorate materially.
The temporary withdrawal pause is a real practical caveat, but the evidence does not show a persistent liquidity shortage or realized market insolvency.

metrics.name: Collateral dependency robustness
metrics.desc:
Quality of admitted collateral assets,High for dominant balances
Liquidity quality of admitted collateral,Strong for major BTC/ETH collateral
Share of collateral in blue-chip assets,High
Share of collateral in long-tail or thinly traded assets,Low on current balances
Reliance on illiquid collateral for solvency,Low
Reliance on fragile collateral structures,Moderate due to LST/LRT exposure
Collateral quality can materially impair solvency,Yes
Fallback protections,Collateral factors, liquidation factors, liquidation penalties, supply caps, reserves, and liquidation mechanisms
Historical evidence of collateral resilience during stress,Yes
Historical evidence of collateral-driven market impairment,No material USDC market impairment identified

metrics.name: Collateral dependency robustness
metrics.desc:
Collateral quality is generally strong because dominant backing is BTC/ETH-linked and liquid.
Concentration and correlated exposure remain meaningful.
The modifier should be mildly punitive rather than heavily punitive.

metrics.name: Collateral and liquidity diversification
metrics.desc:
Diversity of collateral backing the market,Moderate
Concentration in top collateral assets,Top 4 collateral assets ~93.84%
Exposure to correlated collateral,BTC-linked collateral ~60.30%; ETH/LST/LRT-linked collateral ~38.49%
Diversity of borrower base,Not publicly disclosed
Borrow concentration,Historical whale-sized positions identified
Diversity of liquidity exit paths,Moderate
Failure of one collateral group materially disruptive,Yes, potentially
Failure of one major borrower materially disruptive,Possible but not quantified
Public disclosure of concentration risks,Partial
Historical evidence of concentration-driven stress,Partial

metrics.name: Collateral and liquidity diversification
metrics.desc:
The market is diversified by number of listed collateral assets, but economically concentrated in BTC/ETH-linked collateral.
Correlation is the main diversification weakness.
Borrower concentration is not fully disclosed, so the score should remain conservative.

metrics.name: Market monitoring
metrics.desc:
Public dashboards available,Yes
Monitoring of utilization and available cash,Yes
Monitoring of liquidation activity,Yes / partial
Monitoring of reserves or bad debt,Yes / partial
Monitoring of borrower concentration,Partial
Monitoring of collateral concentration,Yes / partial
Monitoring of withdrawal conditions,Yes
Incident response tooling disclosed,Yes / partial
Observability documentation,Limited
Monitoring public, internal, or both,Both
Evidence of monitoring supporting market risk management,Yes

metrics.name: Market monitoring
metrics.desc:
The market is materially observable through public dashboards, governance updates, and risk provider analysis.
Monitoring is strongest for utilization, liquidity, collateral, reserves, and stress behavior.
Borrower concentration and some operational details remain less fully disclosed.

metrics.name: Market incidents history
metrics.desc:
Major market-specific incidents identified,Yes, Nov. 2025 deUSD / sdeUSD-related pause affected the Ethereum USDC market
Historic bad debt events identified,No material USDC market bad debt identified
Historic withdrawal or liquidity incidents identified,Yes, temporary withdrawal pause during stress
Public disclosure quality,High
Post-mortems available,Partial
Time since last major incident,Short
Evidence of repeated market-specific risk pattern,Collateral / oracle configuration and operational response remain recurring themes
Incidents resulted in insolvency or contained disruption,Contained disruption
Evidence of effective remediation after incidents,Yes / partial

metrics.name: Market incidents history
metrics.desc:
No market insolvency was identified.
The incident record is not clean because withdrawal functionality was temporarily constrained during stress.
The right treatment is a modest assurance benefit, not a strong one.

metrics.name: Market configuration maturity
metrics.desc:
Initial deployment date of relevant market architecture,Aug. 17, 2022
Time in production under meaningful usage,Long
Current configuration recent or long-lived,Long-lived
Material changes to collateral framework,Yes
Material changes to parameterization,Yes
Material changes to collateral factors or liquidation settings,Yes
Number of market cycles survived,Multiple
Evidence of stable operation under scale,Yes
Evidence of stable operation during severe volatility,Yes
Current market configuration materially different from early versions,Yes, due to collateral, oracle, and risk-parameter changes

metrics.name: Market configuration maturity
metrics.desc:
The market has meaningful production history and scale.
It has operated through multiple stressed episodes without identified insolvency.
Ongoing parameter and collateral changes prevent a stronger maturity benefit.
